Description: Always use QQC2 controls for consistent native-looking UI
Do: Use Qt Quick Controls 2 (Material / Universal / Fusion style)
Don't: Roll your own primitive controls from scratch
Code Bad: Rectangle { MouseArea { } } as a fake button

Description: Single-responsibility principle for QML files
Do: Split large QML files into focused reusable components
Don't: 500+ line monolithic QML files
Code Bad: One QML file with all screens and logic

Description: Defer creation of heavy components until needed
Do: Loader with source or sourceComponent for off-screen content
Don't: Create all components eagerly at startup

Description: Use id only within the same QML file scope
Do: Use id for intra-file references; pass objects via properties
Don't: Access parent.parent.parent chains
Code Good: Item { id: root }; use root.width inside same file

Description: Enforce required data at instantiation time
Do: Mark mandatory properties with required keyword (Qt 5.15+)
Don't: Use undefined checks at runtime
Code Bad: property string title // silently undefined if not set

Description: Anchor-free responsive layouts with ColumnLayout / RowLayout / GridLayout
Do: Qt Quick Layouts for dynamic resizing and alignment
Don't: Manual x/y/width/height positioning for everything

Description: Anchors for positioning one item relative to its parent or sibling
Do: anchors.fill: parent or anchors.centerIn: parent for simple placement
Don't: Mix anchors and Layout properties on the same item

Description: Switch between full-screen views with StackLayout and currentIndex
Do: StackLayout for tab-based or wizard-style navigation
Don't: Manual visible/opacity toggling on multiple items

Description: Account for notch and system UI insets
Do: Use SafeArea attached properties or Screen.desktopAvailableHeight
Don't: Fixed margins that hide under notch or navigation bar
Code Bad: Item { anchors.fill: parent } without inset compensation

Description: Consistent spacing using a shared constants object
Do: Define spacing tokens (4 / 8 / 16 / 24 / 32) in a singleton
Don't: Magic numbers scattered throughout QML files

Description: Apply a coherent built-in style for all controls
Do: Set QT_QUICK_CONTROLS_STYLE=Material or Universal at app start
Don't: Mix styles in the same app or override individual controls without plan
Code Bad: Manually recreating Material look with raw Rectangles

Guideline: Define theme in a singleton QML
Description: Centralise colors fonts and sizes in a Theme singleton
Do: Theme.qml with pragma Singleton for design tokens
Don't: Hardcode color strings or pixel values in every component

Description: Respond to system dark/light preference
Do: Use Qt.colorScheme / palette to switch themes
Don't: Hardcode dark colors with no light variant

Guideline: Use SVG or Qt vector icons
Description: Scale-independent icons that look crisp on HiDPI
Do: Qt Quick's Image with SVG source or icon font (Material Icons)
Don't: PNG raster icons that blur on HiDPI displays

Description: Corner radius from design tokens; avoid mixing sharp and rounded
Do: Single cornerRadius token used everywhere
Don't: Rectangle { radius: Theme.radiusMd }
Code Good: radius: 3 vs radius: 8 vs radius: 0 across components

Description: Push/pop page model for drill-down flows
Do: StackView for master-detail and drill-down navigation
Don't: Swap visibility manually on multiple pages

Description: Swipeable peer navigation with bottom or top tab bar
Do: SwipeView with TabBar for top-level sections
Don't: Nested SwipeViews at the same hierarchy level
Code Good: SwipeView { id: swipeView }; TabBar { currentIndex: swipeView.currentIndex }
Code Bad: Multiple SwipeViews stacked inside each other

Description: Slide-in drawer for settings and secondary flows
Do: Drawer for auxiliary navigation; keep primary nav in TabBar
Don't: Open drawer for primary actions
Code Good: Drawer { id: navDrawer; width: 240; height: parent.height }
Code Bad: Drawer used as main app navigation replacing TabBar

Description: Intercept Escape / Android back key at the right scope
Do: Keys.onBackPressed or ApplicationWindow close handler for back navigation
Don't: Let the OS close the window without prompt or cleanup
Code Bad: No back-key handling; app closes unexpectedly

Description: Show current location for 3+ level deep navigation
Do: Use a breadcrumb strip or header title stack in StackView
Don't: Leave users disoriented with no location hint
Code Good: StackView currentItem has title property shown in header; header shows back arrow + title
Code Bad: No visual indication of depth or current page

Description: Decouple layers with signals and slots instead of direct calls
Do: Expose C++ slots callable from QML; emit signals to notify QML
Don't: Directly manipulate QML objects from C++ via findChild

Description: Expose C++ properties to QML via Q_PROPERTY with NOTIFY
Do: Q_PROPERTY with READ WRITE NOTIFY for reactive bindings
Don't: Polling C++ from QML in a Timer
Code Good: Q_PROPERTY(int count READ count NOTIFY countChanged)
Code Bad: QTimer polling every 200ms to read C++ value

Description: Prefer Connections type over imperative signal.connect()
Do: Connections { target: backend; function onDataReady() {} }
Don't: Imperative myObj.signal.connect(handler) scattered in JS
Code Good: Connections { target: model; function onRowsInserted() { listView.forceLayout() } }

Description: Ensure property bindings have no circular dependencies
Do: Use Binding object or explicit assignment to break cycles
Don't: Two properties that mutually bind each other
Code Good: Binding { target: slider; property: ""value""; value: spinBox.value }

Description: Reuse delegate instances to reduce allocation churn
Do: Set reuseItems: true on ListView (Qt 5.15+)
Don't: New delegate created and destroyed on every scroll
Code Good: ListView { reuseItems: true; delegate: ItemDelegate { onReused: reset() } }
Code Bad: ListView with no reuseItems; heavy delegates

Description: C++ model is faster than QML ListModel for 100+ items
Do: Subclass QAbstractListModel in C++ for dynamic datasets
Don't: QML ListModel with 500+ dynamically inserted items
Code Bad: ListModel { id: bigModel } with 500 ListElement items

Guideline: Avoid JS in bindings critical path
Description: Minimize JavaScript in property bindings; prefer pure QML expressions
Do: Simple expressions in bindings; move heavy logic to C++
Don't: Complex JS functions called in frequently-evaluated bindings

Description: Load images off the main thread to keep UI responsive
Do: Image asynchronous: true for non-critical images; show placeholder
Don't: Synchronously load all images in Component.onCompleted
Code Good: Image { source: url; asynchronous: true; fillMode: Image.PreserveAspectCrop }

Description: Avoid layer.enabled on many items; layers cost GPU memory
Do: Use layer.enabled only for complex effect compositing
Don't: Apply layer.enabled to every list delegate
Code Good: Item { layer.enabled: true; layer.effect: DropShadow {} } on the single card
Code Bad: layer.enabled: true on every list delegate

Description: Fewer active bindings per delegate = faster scrolling
Do: Cache delegate height; avoid complex expression chains in delegate body
Don't: Dynamic font metrics or heavy operations in delegate bindings
Code Bad: delegate: ItemDelegate { height: textMetrics.height + 20; text: model.name.toUpperCase() }

Description: Attach smooth animations to property changes declaratively
Do: Behavior on value for simple spring-like property animations
Don't: Manually starting/stopping PropertyAnimation imperatively
Code Good: Behavior on opacity { NumberAnimation { duration: 200; easing.type: Easing.OutCubic } }

Description: Declare UI states and animate between them declaratively
Do: states + transitions for discrete UI state changes (open/closed)
Don't: Manually toggling multiple properties from JS
Code Good: states: [ State { name: "open"; PropertyChanges { target: panel; height: 200 } } ]; transitions: Transition { NumberAnimation {} }

Description: Micro-interaction duration that feels snappy but smooth
Do: NumberAnimation duration: 200 for entry; 150 for exit
Don't: Animations over 500ms for routine UI feedback
Code Bad: NumberAnimation { duration: 800 } for a button press

Description: GPU-accelerated properties for smooth 60fps
Do: Use x y scale rotation opacity for animations
Don't: Animate width height anchors causing layout re-evaluation
Code Good: NumberAnimation { target: card; property: "x"; to: 0; duration: 250 }

Description: Disable or reduce animations when accessibility setting is on
Do: Check Qt.styleHints.reducedMotionEnabled and skip Behaviors
Don't: Always run animations regardless of accessibility settings
Code Good: Behavior on opacity { enabled: !Qt.styleHints.reducedMotionEnabled; NumberAnimation { duration: 200 } }
Code Bad: Behavior on opacity { NumberAnimation { duration: 200 } } always active

Guideline: Add accessible name to all controls
Description: Screen readers announce the Accessible.name of each element
Do: Set Accessible.name on every interactive item and image
Don't: Leave controls with no accessible description
Code Bad: Button { text: "" } with no accessible name

Description: Communicate widget semantics to assistive technology
Do: Set Accessible.role matching the control's function (Button Slider etc.)
Don't: Default role for all elements regardless of their function

Description: All interactive elements reachable by Tab and arrow keys
Do: activeFocusOnTab: true; handle Keys.onReturnPressed
Don't: Mouse-only flows with no keyboard equivalent
Code Bad: MouseArea only with no keyboard interaction support

Description: Meet platform tap-target guidelines for touchscreen deployments
Do: Ensure interactive areas are at least 44×44 logical pixels
Don't: Small 16×16 icons with no hit-area expansion

Description: Text must scale with system font size setting
Do: Use sp units via Qt's logical pixel system; avoid fixed pixel fonts
Don't: Fixed pixel font sizes that ignore system scaling

Description: Validate input at the QML level before C++ processing
Do: IntValidator DoubleValidator or RegularExpressionValidator on inputs
Don't: Accept all input and validate only in C++

Description: Display validation errors near the offending field
Do: Text with error color below field; update on editingFinished
Don't: Show all errors in a dialog at the end
Code Good: Column { TextField {}; Text { visible: !valid; color: Theme.errorColor; text: qsTr("Required") } }
Code Bad: Dialog { text: allErrors.join('\n') } shown on submit

Description: Manage focus with FocusScope to avoid global Tab order issues
Do: Wrap logical form groups in FocusScope
Don't: Let focus leak across unrelated UI regions
Code Good: FocusScope { id: loginForm; focus: true; Column { TextField {} TextField {} } }
Code Bad: All TextFields in root scope with uncontrolled Tab order

Description: Prevent double submission with button disabled state
Do: Set Button enabled: false while a backend call is in progress
Don't: Allow re-clicking submit while request is pending

Description: Expose backend services as QML singletons for easy import
Do: Register C++ class as singleton with qmlRegisterSingletonType
Don't: Pass objects through deeply nested property chains

Description: Qt 6 native type registration without manual qmlRegisterType
Do: Add QML_ELEMENT in C++ class and CMake qml_add_module
Don't: Old qmlRegisterType<> calls in main.cpp
Code Good: class MyModel : public QAbstractListModel { Q_OBJECT; QML_ELEMENT }

Description: Never access QML-created objects from C++ threads
Do: Emit signals from background threads; update QML only on main thread
Don't: Set QML property values directly from worker threads
Code Good: emit dataReady(result) from worker thread; Connections receives on main thread

Guideline: Use logical pixels not physical pixels
Description: Qt automatically scales logical pixels; use them everywhere
Do: Logical pixel values for all sizes; let Qt handle device pixel ratio
Don't: Physical pixel math (width * devicePixelRatio) in QML layout
Code Good: width: 48 (logical pixels; Qt scales for Retina/HiDPI)

Description: Supply high-resolution image variants for sharp rendering on HiDPI
Do: Use Qt's @2x suffix convention for auto-selection
Don't: Single 1x PNG for all densities
Code Bad: Image { source: ""icon.png"" } with no HiDPI variant

Guideline: Use Qt Quick Test for QML unit tests
Description: Automated QML component testing in CI
Do: TestCase in QML with Qt Quick Test runner
Don't: Manual visual testing only
Code Bad: No automated test coverage for QML components

Description: Real device rendering differs from desktop preview
Do: Test on actual hardware (embedded / mobile) with target DPI
Don't: Assume desktop simulator matches target device
Code Good: Run on Raspberry Pi / Qt for Device Creation build for embedded
Code Bad: Deploy only to Qt Creator simulator

Guideline: Use qsTr for all visible strings
Description: Support future translations from day one
Do: Wrap all user-visible strings in qsTr()
Don't: Hardcoded English strings in QML

Description: Right-to-left language support via mirroring
Do: LayoutMirroring.enabled: Qt.application.layoutDirection === Qt.RightToLeft
Don't: Hardcoded left-anchors that break RTL
Code Good: LayoutMirroring { enabled: Qt.application.layoutDirection === Qt.RightToLeft; childrenInherit: true }
Code Bad: No RTL support; layout breaks for Arabic/Hebrew
